display.purpose: Primary mobile screen questions and major LINE share headlines only.

headline.purpose: Screen titles, progress summaries, and important challenge state.

title.purpose: Module headings, card titles, and button labels.

label.purpose: Status labels, day chips, helper metadata, and compact state text.

shadows.name: soft-teal-lift
shadows.purpose: Supportive progress modules or focused action containers.

shadows.name: preview-card-lift
shadows.purpose: LINE preview compositions or presentation mockups.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default state transitions for buttons and progress feedback.

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: State changes after check-in or encouragement.

components.name: Primary Check-In Button
components.description: Use for the single highest-intent action on the screen.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary">Check in วันนี้</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #F26D5B; color: #FFFFFF; border: 0; border-radius: 18px; padding: 20px 24px; min-height: 68px; width: 100%; font-family: "LINE Seed Sans TH", "Noto Sans Thai", "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Prompt", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 780; letter-spacing: 0; transition: transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #E95F4D; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #0F5F62; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); }

components.name: Secondary Encouragement Button
components.description: Use for supportive secondary actions such as encouragement or viewing group progress.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #FFFFFF; color: #159A9C; border: 2px solid #159A9C; border-radius: 16px; padding: 14px 20px; min-height: 52px; font-family: "LINE Seed Sans TH", "Noto Sans Thai", "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Prompt", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 760; letter-spacing: 0; transition: background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #E0F4F1; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #F26D5B; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Use for checked-in, not-yet, day count, and group momentum status.
components.html: ไปต่อแล้ว
components.css: .ds-chip-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: #BFEDEA; color: #0F5F62; border-radius: 999px; padding: 6px 10px; font-family: "LINE Seed Sans TH", "Noto Sans Thai", "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Prompt", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0; } .ds-chip-status svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; fill: none; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 2.4; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; }

components.name: Progress Module
components.description: Use to show group movement before individual judgment.
components.html:
Day 6 of 21

กลุ่มกำลังขยับไปต่อ

กลุ่มของเรามี 4/7 คน check in แล้ว
components.css: .ds-progress-card { background: #FFFFFF; color: #2F2A25; border: 1px solid #E8DED2; border-radius: 18px; padding: 24px; font-family: "LINE Seed Sans TH", "Noto Sans Thai", "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Prompt", system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-progress-card h3 { margin: 6px 0 6px; font-size: 20px; line-height: 1.3; font-weight: 720; letter-spacing: 0; } .ds-progress-card p { margin: 0; color: #756F68; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.6; } .ds-progress-kicker { color: #F26D5B !important; font-size: 13px !important; font-weight: 760 !important; } .ds-progress-track { margin-top: 18px; height: 14px; border-radius: 999px; background: #EEE9E3; overflow: hidden; } .ds-progress-track span { display: block; width: 63%; height: 100%; border-radius: inherit; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #0F5F62, #159A9C); }

components.name: Member Status Row
components.description: Use to show member state without ranking or failure framing.
components.html: มมายด์ไปต่อแล้ว
components.css: .ds-member-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; min-height: 56px; padding: 10px 12px; background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E8DED2; border-radius: 14px; color: #2F2A25; font-family: "LINE Seed Sans TH", "Noto Sans Thai", "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Prompt", system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-member-avatar { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 34px; height: 34px; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #159A9C; color: #159A9C; font-weight: 760; } .ds-member-name { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 650; } .ds-member-state { margin-left: auto; color: #0F5F62; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Use for optional proof or setup fields, never as required daily friction unless validated.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 8px; color: #2F2A25; font-family: "LINE Seed Sans TH", "Noto Sans Thai", "IBM Plex Sans Thai", "Prompt", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 650; } .ds-field input { min-height: 48px; border: 1px solid #E8DED2; border-radius: 12px; background: #FFFFFF; color: #2F2A25; padding: 0 16px; font: inherit; font-weight: 400; } .ds-field input::placeholder { color: #756F68; opacity: 1; } .ds-field input:focus { border-color: #159A9C; outline: 3px solid rgba(21, 154, 156, 0.22); outline-offset: 0; }

narrative.overview:
Tabbit's interface should feel like a small group visibly moving through a challenge together. The product is warm and friendly, but its visual energy comes from progress, day count, and shared movement rather than passive coziness or decorative cuteness.

This is a restrained product UI with one clear action layer. Surfaces stay calm and readable for Thai LINE-first mobile use; Momentum Teal carries progress and trust, while Action Coral appears only where the user should act or where a milestone needs energy.

The system explicitly rejects generic productivity dashboards, solo habit tracker patterns, leaderboard-first comparison, shame-based discipline, passive cozy tracking, and rabbit visuals where decoration is clearer than progress.
keyCharacteristics: Mobile-first, thumb-friendly, and readable in a low-attention LINE context.
Progress-oriented: users should understand today's group movement within two seconds.
Supportive: missed-day states are neutral return paths, not failure moments.
Restrained: teal is the memory color, coral is the action signal, and every color must earn its place.
Familiar product UI: standard buttons, cards, chips, and progress states before novelty.

rules.name: The Coral Is Action Rule
rules.body: Action Coral is reserved for CTAs, day counts, milestones, and completion. Do not use coral as confetti, random decoration, or failure.

rules.name: The Teal Carries Momentum Rule
rules.body: Momentum Teal should identify progress, checked-in states, and group movement across every screen.

rules.name: The No Red Failure Rule
rules.body: Missed days use neutral gray, copy, and layout to invite return. Never use harsh red, broken streak visuals, or public failure marks.

rules.name: The Thai-First Clarity Rule
rules.body: User-facing copy must be short, natural Thai with English product terms only when they are clearer, such as check in, progress, MVP, or challenge.

rules.name: The No Display Labels Rule
rules.body: Do not use display-scale type for buttons, chips, labels, member rows, or compact product panels.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Product surfaces are bordered or tonal at rest. Do not pair a 1px border with a broad decorative drop shadow on ordinary cards.

rules.name: The Shadow Must Explain State Rule
rules.body: If a shadow does not clarify focus, action, or preview depth, remove it.

dos: Do make group progress visible within two seconds on the main screen.
Do use Momentum Teal for progress, checked-in states, and active group movement.
Do reserve Action Coral for the main CTA, day count, milestones, and completion moments.
Do keep check-in as one obvious primary action with a large mobile touch target.
Do pair color states with labels, icons, or shape so progress does not rely on color alone.
Do use Thai-first, short, forwardable copy for member-facing moments.
Do keep missed-day states quiet, neutral, and return-oriented.
donts: Don't make Tabbit look like a generic productivity app or to-do list.
Don't make it feel like a solo habit tracker with social features added later.
Don't use leaderboard-first layouts, rankings, trophies, or comparison as the default progress model.
Don't use shame-based discipline, harsh red failure states, broken streak visuals, or public failure marks.
Don't make the product a passive cozy tracker that is admired but does not create action.
Don't make rabbit visuals decorative when they do not communicate progress, commitment, or group movement.
Don't build a broad community platform, full productivity suite, or corporate dashboard in the MVP visual language.
Don't use nested cards, oversized decorative shadows, gradient text, side-stripe accents, or rounded cards above 18px.
